heading: Additional requirements for organizations described in paragraph (9) or (17) of section 501(c)
label: 505 Additional requirements for organizations described in paragraph (9) or (17) of section 501(c)

L. 100–647, § 1011B(a)(32)1986—Subsec. (a)(1). , struck out “of an employer” before “shall”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1851(c)(1)Subsec. (a)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any organization which is part of a plan maintained pursuant to 1 or more collective bargaining agreements between 1 or more employee organizations and 1 or more employers.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1851(c)(4)Subsec. (b)(1). , (3), substituted “as otherwise provided in this subsection” for “as provided in paragraph (2)” in introductory provision, and in subpar. (B) substituted “highly compensated individuals” for “highly compensated employees”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1851(c)(2)Subsec. (b)(2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (2) read as follows: “For purposes of paragraph (1), there may be excluded from consideration—Pub. L. 99–514, § 1151(g)(6)“(A) employees who have not completed 3 years of service,“(B) employees who have not attained age 21,“(C) seasonal employees or less than half-time employees,“(D) employees not included in the plan who are included in a unit of employees covered by an agreement between employee representatives and 1 or more employers which the Secretary finds to be a collective bargaining agreement if the class of benefits involved was the subject of good faith bargaining between such employee representatives and such employer or employers, and“(E) employees who are nonresident aliens and who receive no earned income (within the meaning of section 911(d)(2)) from the employer which constitutes income from sources within the United States (within the meaning of section 861(a)(3)).”Subsec. (b)(4). , amended par. (4) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (4) read as follows: “For purposes of this subsection—Pub. L. 99–514, § 1151(e)(2)(B)“(A) .—At the election of the employer, 2 or more plans of such employer may be treated as 1 plan.Aggregation of plans“(B) .—Rules similar to the rules of subsections (b), (c), (m), and (n) of section 414 shall apply. For purposes of the preceding sentence, section 414(n) shall be applied without regard to paragraph (5).”Treatment of related employersSubsec. (b)(5). , amended par. (5) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (5) read as follows: “For purposes of this subsection, the term ‘highly compensated individual’ has the meaning given such term by section 105(h)(5). For purposes of the preceding sentence, section 105(h)(5) shall be applied by substituting ‘10 percent’ for ‘25 percent’.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 1114(b)(16)Subsec. (b)(6). , added par. (6).Pub. children.heading: Certain requirements must be met in the case of organizations described in section 501(c)(9)
children.label: a Certain requirements must be met in the case of organizations described in section 501(c)(9)

children.heading: Voluntary employees’ beneficiary associations, etc.
children.label: 1 Voluntary employees’ beneficiary associations, etc.
children.text: An organization described in section 501(c)(9) which is part of a plan shall not be exempt from tax under section 501(a) unless such plan meets the requirements of subsection (b) of this section.

children.heading: Exception for collective bargaining agreements
children.label: 2 Exception for collective bargaining agreements
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any organization which is part of a plan maintained pursuant to an agreement between employee representatives and 1 or more employers if the Secretary finds that such agreement is a collective bargaining agreement and that such plan was the subject of good faith bargaining between such employee representatives and such employer or employers.

children.label: A
children.text: each class of benefits under the plan is provided under a classification of employees which is set forth in the plan and which is found by the Secretary not to be discriminatory in favor of employees who are highly compensated individuals, and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of each class of benefits, such benefits do not discriminate in favor of employees who are highly compensated individuals.

children.label: A
children.text: employees who have not completed 3 years of service,

children.label: B
children.text: employees who have not attained age 21,

children.label: C
children.text: seasonal employees or less than half-time employees,

children.label: D
children.text: employees not included in the plan who are included in a unit of employees covered by an agreement between employee representatives and 1 or more employers which the Secretary finds to be a collective bargaining agreement if the class of benefits involved was the subject of good faith bargaining between such employee representatives and such employer or employers, and

children.label: E
children.text: employees who are nonresident aliens and who receive no earned income (within the meaning of section 911(d)(2)) from the employer which constitutes income from sources within the United States (within the meaning of section 861(a)(3)).

children.heading: Application of subsection where other nondiscrimination rules provided
children.label: 3 Application of subsection where other nondiscrimination rules provided
children.text: In the case of any benefit for which a provision of this chapter other than this subsection provides nondiscrimination rules, paragraph (1) shall not apply but the requirements of this subsection shall be met only if the nondiscrimination rules so provided are satisfied with respect to such benefit.

children.heading: Aggregation rules
children.label: 4 Aggregation rules
children.text: At the election of the employer, 2 or more plans of such employer may be treated as 1 plan for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Highly compensated individual
children.label: 5 Highly compensated individual
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the determination as to whether an individual is a highly compensated individual shall be made under rules similar to the rules for determining whether an individual is a highly compensated employee (within the meaning of section 414(q)).

children.heading: Compensation
children.label: 6 Compensation
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “compensation” has the meaning given such term by section 414(s).

children.heading: Compensation limit
children.label: 7 Compensation limit
children.text: A plan shall not be treated as meeting the requirements of this subsection unless under the plan the annual compensation of each employee taken into account for any year does not exceed $200,000. The Secretary shall adjust the $200,000 amount at the same time, and by the same amount, as any adjustment under section 401(a)(17)(B). This paragraph shall not apply in determining whether the requirements of section 79(d) are met.

children.heading: Requirement that organization notify Secretary that it is applying for tax-exempt status
children.label: c Requirement that organization notify Secretary that it is applying for tax-exempt status
A
children.text: unless it has given notice to the Secretary, in such manner as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe, that it is applying for recognition of such status, or

children.label: B
children.text: for any period before the giving of such notice, if such notice is given after the time prescribed by the Secretary by regulations for giving notice under this subsection.

children.heading: Special rule for existing organizations
children.label: 2 Special rule for existing organizations
children.text: In the case of any organization in existence on , the time for giving notice under paragraph (1) shall not expire before the date 1 year after such date of the enactment.July 18, 1984
